name: aiida-submission-controller

metadata.name: aiida-submission-controller
metadata.summary: Utilities for controlling submission flow in AiiDA

documentation:
# AiiDA submission controller

Some classes to help managing large number of submissions, while controlling the maximum number of submissions running at any given time.

This project is still in early phase and the API might change.

It includes an abstract base class that implements the main logic, a very simple example of an implementation
to compute a 12x12 addition table (in `examples/add_in_batches.py`), and a main script to run it (and get results and show them).

To use it, you are supposed to launch the script (e.g. in a `screen` terminal) with something like this:
```bash
cd examples
while true ; do verdi run add_in_batches.py ; sleep 5 ; done
```
where you can adapt the sleep time.
Typically, for real simulations, you might want something in the
range of 5-10 minutes, or anyway so that at every new run you have at least some new processes to submit,
but still less that the maximum number of available slots, to try to keep the 'queue' quite filled at any
given time.

There is also a second subclass that, rather than just creating new submissions from some extras, will use (input) nodes in another group as a reference for which calculations to run (e.g.: a group of crystal structures, representing the inputs to a set of workflows).

modules.name: aiida_submission_controller
documentation: A prototype package with tools to submit processes in batches, avoiding to submit too many.
-*- coding: utf-8 -*-

modules.name: aiida_submission_controller.from_group
documentation: A prototype class to submit processes in batches, avoiding to submit too many.
-*- coding: utf-8 -*-

classes.name: aiida_submission_controller.from_group.FromGroupSubmissionController

methods.name: aiida_submission_controller.from_group.FromGroupSubmissionController.parent_group
documentation: Return the AiiDA ORM Group instance of the parent group.

methods.name: aiida_submission_controller.from_group.FromGroupSubmissionController.get_parent_node_from_extras
documentation: Return the Node instance (in the parent group) from the (unique) extras identifying it.

methods.name: aiida_submission_controller.from_group.FromGroupSubmissionController.get_all_extras_to_submit
documentation:
Return a *set* of the values of all extras uniquely identifying all simulations that you want to submit.

Each entry of the set must be a tuple, in same order as the keys returned by get_extra_unique_keys().

They are taken from the extra_unique_keys from the group.
Note: the extra_unique_keys must actually form a unique set;
if this is not the case, an AssertionError will be raised.

class_variables.name: aiida_submission_controller.from_group.FromGroupSubmissionController.parent_group_label
documentation: Label of the parent group from which to construct the process inputs.

class_variables.name: aiida_submission_controller.from_group.FromGroupSubmissionController.filters
documentation: Filters applied to the query of the nodes in the parent group.

class_variables.name: aiida_submission_controller.from_group.FromGroupSubmissionController.order_by
documentation: Ordering applied to the query of the nodes in the parent group.

SubmissionController implementation getting data to submit from a parent group.

This is (still) an abstract base class: you need to subclass it
and define the abstract methods.

modules.name: aiida_submission_controller.base
documentation: A prototype class to submit processes in batches, avoiding to submit too many.
-*- coding: utf-8 -*-

functions.name: aiida_submission_controller.base.get_extras_dict
documentation: Return a dictionary of extras from a list of keys and a list of values.

functions.name: aiida_submission_controller.base.validate_group_exists
documentation: Validator that makes sure the `Group` with the provided label exists.

classes.name: aiida_submission_controller.base.BaseSubmissionController

methods.name: aiida_submission_controller.base.BaseSubmissionController.group
documentation: Return the AiiDA ORM Group instance that is managed by this class.

methods.name: aiida_submission_controller.base.BaseSubmissionController.get_query
documentation:
Return a QueryBuilder object to get all processes in the group associated to this.

Projections on the process must be specified.

```
note

the query has the process already tagged with tag "process", so you can further append to this query
using this tag, e.g. to get the outputs of the process itself.

param process_projections

a list of projections for the ProcessNode.

param only_active

if True, will filter only on active (not-sealed) processes.
```

methods.name: aiida_submission_controller.base.BaseSubmissionController.get_process_extra_projections
documentation:
Return a list of QueryBuilder projections on the process.

They will return the values of the extras according to the output of `get_extra_unique_keys()`
(in the same order).

The idea is to be used as `process_projections` for `get_query()`.

methods.name: aiida_submission_controller.base.BaseSubmissionController.get_all_submitted_pks
documentation:
Return a dictionary of all processes that have been already submitted (i.e., are in the group).

```
return

a dictionary where:

the keys are the tuples with the values of extras that uniquely identifies processes, in the same
order as returned by get_extra_unique_keys().

the values are the corresponding process PKs.

note

this returns all processes, both active and completed (sealed).
```

methods.name: aiida_submission_controller.base.BaseSubmissionController.get_all_submitted_processes
documentation:
Return a dictionary of all processes that have been already submitted (i.e., are in the group).

```
return

a dictionary where:

the keys are the tuples with the values of extras that uniquely identifies processes, in the same
order as returned by get_extra_unique_keys().

the values are the corresponding AiiDA ProcessNode instances.

note

this returns all processes, both active and completed (sealed).
```

methods.name: aiida_submission_controller.base.BaseSubmissionController._check_submitted_extras
documentation: Return a set with the extras of the processes tha have been already submitted.

methods.name: aiida_submission_controller.base.BaseSubmissionController._count_active_in_group
documentation: Count how many active (unsealed) processes there are in the group.

methods.name: aiida_submission_controller.base.BaseSubmissionController.num_active_slots
documentation: Number of active slots (i.e. processes in the group that are unsealed).

methods.name: aiida_submission_controller.base.BaseSubmissionController.num_available_slots
documentation: Number of available slots (i.e. how many processes would be submitted in the next batch submission).

methods.name: aiida_submission_controller.base.BaseSubmissionController.num_to_run
documentation: Number of processes that still have to be submitted.

methods.name: aiida_submission_controller.base.BaseSubmissionController.num_already_run
documentation: Number of processes that have already been submitted (and might or might not have finished).

methods.name: aiida_submission_controller.base.BaseSubmissionController.submit_new_batch
documentation: Submit a new batch of calculations, ensuring less than self.max_concurrent active at the same time.

methods.name: aiida_submission_controller.base.BaseSubmissionController.get_extra_unique_keys
documentation: Return a tuple of the keys of the unique extras that will be used to uniquely identify your workchains.

methods.name: aiida_submission_controller.base.BaseSubmissionController.get_all_extras_to_submit
documentation:
Return a *set* of the values of all extras uniquely identifying all simulations that you want to submit.

Each entry of the set must be a tuple, in same order as the keys returned by get_extra_unique_keys().

:note: for each item, pass extra values as tuples (because lists are not hashable, so you cannot make
    a set out of them).

methods.name: aiida_submission_controller.base.BaseSubmissionController.get_inputs_and_processclass_from_extras
documentation:
Return the inputs and the process class for the process to run, associated a given tuple of extras values.

```
param extras_values

a tuple of values of the extras, in same order as the keys returned by
get_extra_unique_keys().

return

(inputs, process_class), that will be used as follows:

submit(process_class, **inputs)

source:8: (WARNING/2) Inline strong start-string without end-string.
```

class_variables.name: aiida_submission_controller.base.BaseSubmissionController.group_label
documentation: Label of the group to store the process nodes in.

class_variables.name: aiida_submission_controller.base.BaseSubmissionController.max_concurrent
documentation: Maximum concurrent active processes.

class_variables.name: aiida_submission_controller.base.BaseSubmissionController.unique_extra_keys
documentation: Tuple of keys defined in the extras that uniquely define each process to be run.

Controller to submit a maximum number of processes (workflows or calculations) at a given time.

This is an abstract base class: you need to subclass it and define the abstract methods.
